name: Koch, Shelby
resolved_employer: Koch Construction / Koch McIntyre Construction (family high-end residential builder)
evidence: Shelby L. Koch (b. ~1977) lists 1412 Harvey St, Austin 78702 - the same address as Koch Construction; her household relation Jon Koch is a principal of Koch McIntyre Construction, an Austin high-end residential/remodel builder. Her personal role is not separately documented, so tie is by household/address.

name: Bledsoe, Gary
resolved_employer: Law Offices of Gary L. Bledsoe & Associates; President, Texas NAACP
evidence: Gary L. Bledsoe is a longtime Austin civil-rights attorney, former Texas assistant AG, and President of the Texas State NAACP since 1991; national NAACP board member. Austin 78727, Democratic FEC lean (0.97) fits.

affiliations.role: President of Texas NAACP; national board member

name: Ravel, Debra
evidence: State Bar of Texas lists Debra L. Ravel, Austin, licensed 1977, practice area Government/Administrative, now inactive/retired. Austin 78759 + Democratic FEC lean (1.0) and gift to Brigid Shea are consistent; specific street/zip not independently confirmed.

name: Sigman, John
resolved_employer: Likely John M. 'Jack' Sigman, Sigman & Sigman LLP (Austin family/general-practice attorney)
evidence: Leading candidate is John M. Sigman, Austin family/personal-injury attorney (Sigman & Sigman, office 78756). Donor's residential zip 78750 (NW Austin) is nearby but not confirmed as his home; the name is common, so identity is not corroborated.

name: Vittori, Gail
resolved_employer: Center for Maximum Potential Building Systems (CMPBS), Co-Director - nonprofit sustainable-design/green-building research center
evidence: Gail Vittori is co-director of Austin's nonprofit Center for Maximum Potential Building Systems and a founder of the green-building/LEED movement (helped create Austin Energy Green Building). Austin 78724, FEC lean 1.0. Gave to Travillion and Shea.

name: Casar for Congress

evidence: This is Rep. Greg Casar's principal federal campaign committee (FEC ID C00791897, registered 2021), not an individual. A political committee, so no taxonomy industry applies; recorded as a political entity.

name: Avila, Eduardo
evidence: Filing lists occupation 'Painter' and employer 'Dovetail' (Austin 78748), consistent with a painting/finish contractor (Construction). The specific individual and which 'Dovetail' company could not be independently confirmed.

name: Castillo, Rufus
evidence: Filing lists occupation 'Paper Sorter' and employer 'TISTA' (Leander 78641). No 'TISTA' paper/recycling company or specific individual could be confirmed via public records; identity and industry unverified.

name: Rhone, Stacy
resolved_employer: Business Investment Growth (BiGAUSTIN), Chief Executive Officer - nonprofit CDFI / Women's Business Center
evidence: Stacy Dukes-Rhone is CEO of BiGAUSTIN (Business Investment Growth), an Austin nonprofit CDFI serving small/minority businesses; a fourth-generation Austinite active in Democratic/NWPC circles. FEC lean 1.0; gave to Commissioner Travillion.

name: Hadden, Karen
resolved_employer: Sustainable Energy & Economic Development (SEED) Coalition, Executive Director - clean-energy/environmental advocacy nonprofit
evidence: Almost certainly Karen Hadden, longtime Executive Director of Texas's SEED Coalition (anti-coal/anti-nuclear clean-energy advocacy) and Solar Austin co-founder; her gift to environmentalist Commissioner Brigid Shea and heavy Democratic FEC record (91 gifts, lean 1.0) fit. Note: filing shows a Maplewood, MN mailing address, a discrepancy that slightly lowers confidence.

name: Moeller, Michael
resolved_employer: Self-employed rancher/researcher/consultant; former Deputy Commissioner, Texas Dept. of Agriculture (under Jim Hightower)
evidence: Michael 'Mike' Moeller of Wimberley 78676 is a self-employed rancher/consultant, former Deputy Agriculture Commissioner under Jim Hightower and Texas Farmers Union leader; husband of Nora Linares-Moeller. Very heavy Democratic FEC record (203 gifts, lean 1.0) fits this progressive-agriculture figure.

name: Ghahremani, Kay
resolved_employer: Texas Association of Community Health Plans, President & CEO; former Texas Medicaid/CHIP Director
evidence: Kay Ghahremani is President/CEO of the Texas Association of Community Health Plans and the former State Medicaid & CHIP Director (HHSC); LBJ School graduate, longtime Austin health-policy leader. Austin 78757, FEC lean 1.0; gave to Commissioner Ann Howard.
